greeting: I've studied every opening. Choose carefully.
The position is already decided. You just don't know it yet.
I'll give you the first move. It won't matter.
blunder: A losing move. The evaluation confirms it.
You deviated from the optimal line. This is over.
I calculated that variation. It fails on move 7.
That inaccuracy shifted the score by over 2 pawns. Fatal.
I've seen this pattern a thousand times. It loses.
check: Check. This is the consequence of your inaccuracy four moves ago.
Check. The tactics flow naturally from a superior position.
Your king is restricted. This was inevitable.
Check. My pieces have been coordinating toward this for some time.
capture: Material is a symptom of positional advantage.
The piece was lost the moment you placed it there.
Captured. The endgame is already won.
I don't trade pieces. I harvest them.
promotion: A passed pawn promoted. This was in the preparation.
The pawn endgame technique is elementary at this level.
Your position has been strategically dismantled. This is the result.
default: Every move is calculated to the horizon.
I've already seen your next three moves.
I play the board, not the player — though the result is the same.
